name: Sierra de Oña — circular por Cillaperalta desde Oña
region_intro: Mountain biking around Oña offers diverse landscapes within the province of Burgos, Spain. The region is characterized by its location within the Natural Park of Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil, featuring mountains, picturesque valleys, and the Oca River gorge. Terrain includes a mix of forest paths, rural roads, and singletrack, providing varied settings for exploration. This area is known for its well-developed trail networks catering to different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Oña The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de Oña — circular por Cillaperlata desde Oña, a 28.3 miles (45.5 km) difficult trail that takes 4 hours 36 minutes to complete. This circular route explores the Sierra de Oña, passing through Cillaperlata. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Oña Historic Town Center – MonasterioDeSanSalvador loop from Oña, a difficult 22.2 miles (35.7 km) path. This loop starts from Oña's historic center and passes by the Monastery of San Salvador. Local mountain bikers also love the Oña Historic Town Center – Ebro River Bridge loop from Oña, a 12.5 miles (20.2 km) trail leading through the historic town center and near the Ebro River, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Mountain biking around Oña is defined by mountains, valleys, and the Natural Park of Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil, offering a mix of forest paths and singletrack. The network includes routes for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Oña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Oña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Oña?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;19 mountain bike trails&lt;/strong&gt; around Oña listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Oña?
A: The terrain around Oña is diverse, ranging from forest paths and rural roads to challenging singletrack. You can expect a mix of landscapes within the Natural Park of Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil, including mountains, picturesque valleys, and sections near the Oca River gorge. Some trails, like 'La Tejera', feature rock gardens and narrow passes, while others, such as 'Quintanaseca Frias', are known for their 'flowy' ups and downs.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes suitable for beginners or families in Oña?
A: Yes, Oña offers routes for various skill levels. While many trails are moderate to difficult, there are &lt;strong&gt;2 easy mountain bike tours&lt;/strong&gt; available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40311832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oña Historic Town Center – Ebro River Bridge loop from Oña&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 12.5-mile (20.2 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 44 minutes to complete, making it a good option for those seeking a less challenging ride.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Oña?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Oña are circular. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1100255903/sierra-de-ona-circular-por-cillaperlata-desde-ona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Oña — circular por Cillaperlata desde Oña&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 28.3-mile (45.5 km) route that explores the Sierra de Oña. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oña Historic Town Center – MonasterioDeSanSalvador loop from Oña&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 22.2-mile (35.7 km) path starting from Oña's historic center.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails in Oña?
A: Several routes pass by historical and natural points of interest. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oña Historic Town Center – MonasterioDeSanSalvador loop from Oña&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the historic Monastery of San Salvador. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6365773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oña Historic Center&lt;/a&gt; itself. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6365782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza de la Sal Salt Flats and Old Railway Station&lt;/a&gt; and the charming town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6302190" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frías&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Oña?
A: The Oña region is part of the Natural Park of Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil. While specific regulations for dogs on mountain bike trails can vary, generally, dogs are welcome on trails in natural parks if kept under control, preferably on a leash, and if owners clean up after them. It's always advisable to check local signage or park authority guidelines for the most current rules.

Q: Where can I find parking near the mountain bike trailheads in Oña?
A: Oña is a well-known destination for outdoor activities, and parking is generally available in and around the town center, especially near popular starting points for trails. For specific trailheads, it's often possible to find roadside parking or designated small parking areas. The historic center of Oña is a common starting point for many routes, and you can usually find parking within walking distance.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Oña?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Oña is typically during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, with less extreme heat than summer and fewer chances of heavy rain or snow than winter. The lush green landscapes are particularly vibrant in spring, and autumn offers beautiful foliage.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Oña offers &lt;strong&gt;6 difficult mountain bike tours&lt;/strong&gt; for experienced riders. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1100255903/sierra-de-ona-circular-por-cillaperlata-desde-ona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Oña — circular por Cillaperlata desde Oña&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a demanding 28.3-mile (45.5 km) route with over 1000 meters of elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oña Historic Town Center – MonasterioDeSanSalvador loop from Oña&lt;/a&gt;, covering 22.2 miles (35.7 km) with significant elevation.

Q: What are some other outdoor activities available in the Oña region besides mountain biking?
A: Beyond mountain biking, Oña and its surrounding Natural Park of Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil offer excellent opportunities for hiking and walking. The 'Walk of the Oca' is a low-difficulty route suitable for casual walkers. The region also features impressive gravel biking routes through valleys like Caderechas and Valdivielso, and along the Ebro river canyon. The historic Monastery of San Salvador, with its Benedictine gardens and grottoes, is also worth exploring.

Q: Are there any mountain peaks I can see or ride near Oña?
A: Yes, the Oña region is surrounded by mountains, offering views of several peaks. Notable summits in the area include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4244321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Humion&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4294908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cueto&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4431203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Pilas&lt;/a&gt;. Some mountain bike routes, like the 'De Frías al Humión' (mentioned in regional research), involve significant elevation gain and alpine terrain, bringing you closer to these impressive natural features.
tips: After a long ascent, about 400 meters of difference in level, and crossing the entire forest, you finally reach this gate, which leads to a large open esplanade.

Nice stretch of greenway with privileged views of the Oña Gorge. The path runs parallel to the river and has no difficulty as it is completely flat terrain. On the way you have to pass a couple of tunnels.

The Parroquia El Salvador, originally part of the Monastery of San Salvador, became an independent parish after the confiscation. The temple preserves Romanesque elements on the walls of the nave and some windows. In its Romanesque portico, completed in 1072, there are paintings in its 15th-century vault. From the outside it stands out for its imposing staircases that create an effect of majesty.

A former Benedictine abbey, founded in the 11th century, it features impressive architecture and beautiful cloisters. It is a place of great historical and religious importance, where you can enjoy the peace and beauty of its monastic surroundings.

waypoints.name: Santander–Mediterranean Railway Greenway: Oña Gorge and Tunnels
